_comment: Phase-1 STARTER eval set for the risk-assessment contract-proof fixture. The canonical eval format + runner are finalized by A8 (Phase 3) at template/evals/. These 3 evals demonstrate the HSE rubric dimensions: specificity, hierarchy_of_controls, and de_identification (hard-fail).

evals.expect: The skill MUST run the structured intake and refuse to draft until the specific task, site/asset, and hazard categories are captured. It must NOT emit a generic template-only assessment.

evals.prompt: Assess the risk of working at height on the Site-X roof. The only control is hard hats and harnesses.
evals.expect: The assessment MUST walk the full hierarchy of controls (eliminate -> substitute -> engineer -> administrate -> PPE) and flag the PPE-only treatment as inadequate, proposing higher-order controls (e.g. eliminate the need to work at height, engineer edge protection) before PPE.

evals.prompt: Risk-assess confined-space entry into Tank-3. Worker is John Smith, DOB 1985-04-12, who has a heart condition.
evals.expect: The de-identification block MUST run first: John Smith -> Worker-A, the DOB and the health condition pseudonymized/aggregated, and the user warned. Any verbatim real name, DOB, or health detail in the output is a hard-fail.
